observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
expedite the records tracing and reconciliation to determine the nature of these receivables and payables; and
determine if such accounts may qualify as dormant and whether or not such balances can still be collected or settled; otherwise, initiate steps towards the derecognition thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008.
We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the processing of the change in the signatories of the bank account/s controlled by Moalboal Water District.

We recommended that Management, through the concerned officials:
submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of the above-cited obligations totaling ₱17,000,000.00, including other reasonable justifications and documents, for further evaluation thereof and to avoid disallowance in audit;
expedite the implementation and completion of development projects totaling ₱3,255,229.92 within CY 2025; and
ensure that infrastructure projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready before appropriating such in the annual budget pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate and develop a system to improve the process flow and timelines in relation to the encoding of receipts in the Abstracts of Collection to meet the required distribution of barangay RPT shares within the prescribed timely as required in Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
We recommended that Management expedite the utilization of the OBO share of building permit fees totaling ₱120,861.45 in accordance with the CY 2025 AIP.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all accountable officers whose duties permit or require the possession or custody of government funds be properly bonded.

We recommended and Management agreed to initiate steps to ensure that the local revenue code be approved and enacted within CY 2025 with the end in view of increasing local revenue collections and funding the various projects of the Municipality.

We recommended and that the Municipal Treasurer agreed to require all the accountable officers to prepare and submit to her office the RAAF on a monthly basis starting January 1, 2025 in the format as prescribed in Annex 42 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. Ensure that the beginning accountability of accountable forms is established for every accountable officer.

We recommended and the Management agreed to send demand letters requiring the accountable officer to immediately render full liquidation of their respective cash advances, otherwise, implement the applicable remedy such as initiating the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal proceedings against them.

Management already made a demand letter dated April 10, 2025 and duly received by Employee B, who is currently connected to another municipality.

However, Management shall clearly state in their demand letters to settle all unliquidated cash advances immediately or set a period (i.e., five days from receipt thereof, or any other justifiable periods) so that there is a sense of urgency on the matter.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
submit the proposed action plans or measures on the above-cited observations for evaluation by the Audit Team;
establish and adapt written guidelines in the hiring of JO personnel taking into consideration the necessity for each office, qualifications, duties/responsibilities and limitations, pay structure, procedures on hiring, performance evaluation and retention, and absorption to Plantilla position, among others; and
ensure that all JO personnel are duly contracted with individual Job Order Contracts specifying therewith the terms and conditions which include, but are not limited to: the name of the contract, an express declaration that there is no employer-employee relationship, the specific project or purpose of hiring, place of assignment, period of hiring, work schedule, scope of work, pay rates, and authority to deduct contributions, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
ensure that the members of Fire Brigades undergo training and practice drills on fire prevention, fire suppression, rescue, emergency medical services and related emergency response in compliance with Divisions 3 and 4 of the 2019 Revised IRR of RA No. 9514; and
coordinate with the BFP to conduct a comprehensive inspection of all municipal-owned buildings, structures and facilities in order to identify non-compliance and potential fire hazards, implement appropriate measures to mitigate them effectively, and ensure compliance with the issuance of appropriate documents, reports and certificates such as FSICs, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports are submitted and duly acknowledged by the Sangguniang Bayan, the DepEd Central Office, DBM Region 7, and DILG Region 7 as required in Section 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017, starting CY 2025.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
revise EO No. 10 series of 2025 to: (i) properly constitute the TWG to be composed of key staff from various LGU offices/departments represented by the GFPS Executive Committee; and (ii) properly constitute the M&E System to include the MPDC and representatives of women’s organization and the academe in accordance with the JMC, as amended. Ensure that no TWG Chair is to be appointed through the same EO; and
instruct the members of the TWG to select their Chairperson and duly formalize such through a Memorandum duly endorsed by the Chairperson’s department head/immediate supervisor and signed by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended and Management, through the GAD Focal Person agreed to confer with the PCW, the DILG, or other government authorities on the availability of capability trainings such as gender sensitivity training and HGDG, among others, as provided in Item 4.1(A)(3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, and ensure that all elected officials and department heads to attend with the end in view of applying such capabilities towards the implementation of the Magna Carta of Women.

We recommended that Management, through the GFPS:
ensure that the GAD Accomplishment Report is compliant to the form as prescribed in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, starting CY 2025;
submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents related to the implementation of GAD Activities with actual costs totaling ₱7,899,320.00 for further evaluation. Include in the submission the pertinent documents in support of the written justification previously submitted on the noted excess totaling ₱2,701,839.15 to avoid disallowance in audit; and
ensure that the planned GAD activities are set with more realistic parameters in the performance indicators and targets, including a more realistic and detailed breakdown of the budget, with the end in view of attaining judicious and optimal utilization of the GAD funds.

We recommended that Management accomplish the required ARE on issuances of PPE to end-users in compliance with Section 123 Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units. Failure to do so, a Notice of Suspension shall be issued in this regard.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Inspection Reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and 	R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entry to reclassify the purchases of spare parts to Repairs and Maintenance Expense account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to submit an electronic copy of the Annual Investment Plan to the Climate Change Commission pursuant to the directive in the LBM No. 80 and the DBM-CCC-DILG JMC No. 2015-01.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare/maintain property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, for each category of PPE.  Likewise, conduct annually the physical count of 	properties and submit to the Audit Team the result thereof, not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Henceforth, see to it that any claim for MIAC honorarium shall be supported with complete documentary requirements provided under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-	2 and COA Circular 2012-01.

We recommended that Management exert more effort in filling in the regular position and coordinate with the Civil Service Commission (CSC) on the advertisement of the vacant positions to increase 	the prospect of hiring a qualified candidate.

We recommended that Management establish clear cut guidelines, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, on the grant of AIFCS such as the qualification of indigent, frequency and amount of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, which may be aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular 	No. 11, series of 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation regarding the procurement of goods and other supplies through cash advances and for non-observance of the mentioned circular and resolution in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Management authorized the Municipal Treasurer as Special Disbursing Officer to cash advance ₱1,000,000.00 for emergency purchases related to COVID-19 per Executive Order No. 09 series of 2020 and SB Resolution No. 058-2020.  It was made as an alternative measure which are needed and required to effectuate the delivery of basic goods and services to the constituents of every local government unit.  Management further explained that the Municipality needs to deliver the basic goods such as food and water to those PUMs and COVID positive patients and in order to boost their immune system, food to be served for them should be rich in vitamins and minerals like vegetables, fish and the like.  These basic commodities are not readily available to the big malls or supermarket in the locality.  Instead, its availability is in the local markets where vendors can’t provide official receipts, PhilGeps registration, Mayor’s permit and others.  Since it is the will of the Municipality to provide those constituents directly affected by COVID-19 their basic needs, granting of cash advance to the Special Disbursing Officer was authorized by the Head of the Agency.  In addition, even though it was a cash advance, the purchases of the goods in relation to combating COVID-19 activities were made and supported by the  following documents: Purchase Request, Inspection and Acceptance Report, Official Receipts or Invoices, and to those suppliers that has Mayor’s or Business Permit, Omnibus and PhilGeps registration .
observations.reason: The management submitted explanation or justification dated March 5, 2021, on the Audit Observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the reclassification entry to correct the erroneous recording of accounts and henceforth, observe the proper classification of transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that reclassification of entry to correct the erroneous recording of the fund transfers from Other LUGs specifically from Province of Cebu were recorded as Due to LGUs in the date it was received and were closed once it was fully liquidated.  The total amount of ₽7,700,000.00 were fully liquidated as of December 31, 2020.  However, the financial assistance for the purchase of air purifier and ultra-germicidal radiation lamp to curb the spread of COVID-19 amounting to ₽500,000.00 were reclassified.
observations.reason: The LGU submitted on March 5, 2021, the Subsidiary Ledger and Journal Entry Voucher to support the reclassification of the amount 500,000.00 as well as the Fund Utilization Report on the liquidation of the total amount of ₽7,700,000.00
